text:
Once upon a time in a quiet village nestled amidst lush green hills, there lived a kind-hearted old milkmaid named Matilda. Every morning before heading out to the farm, Matilda would pour her sunlit milk into a charming wooden bowl, which she lovingly named “Molly.”

One particular spring day, when the mist still lingered, and the world whispered secrets of rebirth, Matilda felt an inexplicable chill. Without realizing it, she carried the bowl instead of her usual water pitcher. As she meandered through the village, the chill seeped inside her, a ghost of a winter’s frost.

In her panic, she rushed to her front door, intending to refill the water jug. But her hands shook so violently that she lost grasp of the bowl. With a gasp, it slipped from her grasp and fluttered down the cobblestone path, only to land squarely on the snow-covered ground.
